forecast-period.text: A broad trough lies over SA. A cold front clips the southeast as an upper trough moves up over SA today and tomorrow. A high pressure system develops south of the Bight late in the week and persists over the weekend.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Southerly about 10 knots increasing to 15 to 20 knots in the middle of the day.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. The chance of a thunderstorm north of Port Vincent to Brighton in the late morning and afternoon.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Gulf St Vincent
forecast-period.text: Southerly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots during the afternoon and evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres around midday.
Southerly 15 to 25 knots tending southeasterly 15 to 20 knots during the evening.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
